pages.text: <name> had always loved the old clock tower at the edge of town. One evening, he noticed a faint tick-tock echoing from inside. Curiosity twinkling in his eyes, he climbed the spiral stairs—and whoosh! suddenly, he stepped into a hidden kingdom filled with glowing gears, dancing cogs, and tiny clockwork creatures.
A kind clockmaker king welcomed him. “Welcome, <name>! But beware—the Great Clock is very old and must keep running, or time stops here.”
The next morning, disaster struck. The Great Clock froze, and the kingdom stood still. Birds hung in midair, and clockwork dancers froze mid-twirl. “The golden spring is missing!” cried the king.

<name> knew he had to help. A clever talking mouse appeared, offering riddles as clues: 'I spin yet do not move, I give life but have no groove. Find me where gold sleeps tight, And time will flow back right.'

<name> followed the riddles through winding corridors, past ticking staircases, and over silver bridges. At the hidden vault, a grumpy merchant blocked the way, hoping to sell the golden spring to the highest bidder. <name> thought fast, offering a shiny gear from the tower as a trade. The merchant grumbled but agreed.

Triumphantly, <name> placed the golden spring back into the Great Clock. Gears spun, pendulums swung, and time flowed again. The kingdom cheered! Clockwork dancers twirled, and the king presented <name> with a tiny, ticking medallion as a token of gratitude.

As <name> stepped back through the tower door into his world, he looked up at the clock. It ticked happily, and he smiled, knowing that courage, clever thinking, and kindness could bring even the trickiest kingdom back to life.
